title: Why Static Evals Decay
concept: How benchmark data leaks into model training sets, and the need for dynamic, generated test sets.

exercises.prompt: Why do we run evaluations in isolated environments for AI agents?

options.text: To prevent agents from learning from the tests.
To have more agents involved in a test.

exercises.explain: We isolate evaluations to stop the AI agents from knowing the test questions in advance, which could skew results.

cards.title: Understanding Static Evaluations
cards.body: Static evaluations are tests that don't change over time. They become less effective if they are reused too often.

cards.title: Data Leakage
cards.body: Data leakage happens when information from the test set leaks into the model's training data, making evaluations misleading.

cards.title: Like a Magic Show
cards.body: If a magician performs the same trick every time, the audience figures out the secret. Similarly, static tests can give away the answers to agents.

cards.title: Example of Decay
cards.body: If an AI repeatedly takes the same test and improves its score, it might just be memorizing the answers instead of learning.

cards.title: Myth: Static Tests Are Always Effective
cards.body: Many believe that static tests are reliable, but they can mislead if agents memorize questions rather than genuinely evaluate their intelligence.

cards.title: Static vs. Dynamic Tests
cards.body: Static tests become predictable over time, while dynamic tests are fresh and varied.

left.text: Repeating the same tests leads to decay.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to avoid data leakage in evaluations?

options.text: It helps the model learn effectively without being biased.
It allows the model to memorize the answers.
It guarantees that all models will perform equally.

exercises.explain: Avoiding data leakage ensures that the AI genuinely learns from new data, rather than just memorizing answers from tests.

exercises.prompt: Dynamic test sets can improve evaluation accuracy. True or False?
exercises.explain: Dynamic test sets introduce new challenges, helping to assess the true learning of AI models.

exercises.prompt: Dynamic tests help ensure evaluations remain __________.
exercises.sentence: Keeping evaluations relevant requires using dynamic test sets.
exercises.explain: Dynamic tests keep evaluations relevant because they prevent memorization and promote true learning.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes a benefit of dynamic evaluations?

options.text: They allow for quicker model training.
They prevent data leakage and keep tests fresh.
They are easier to set up than static tests.

exercises.explain: Dynamic evaluations prevent data leakage and enrich the testing process by continually engaging the model with new information.

exercises.prompt: What is a key reason to use dynamic test sets instead of static evaluations for AI models?

options.text: They reduce the chances of memorization and data leakage.
They are less expensive to implement.

exercises.explain: Dynamic test sets help to significantly reduce memorization and data leakage, leading to a more accurate assessment of the AI's capabilities.
